title: Candidate pairwise positional interaction forward-fix rerun

queries: label token order pooling candidate pairwise scorer scalable multiclass graph llm
cheap token sequence label encoder pairwise ranking multiclass candidate aware scorer

evidence.why_now: EXP135 was still implementation-blocked rather than scientifically resolved because candidate_pairwise_positional_interaction was accidentally routed through the top-k dual hybrid forward path on item-incoterms. The hypothesis remains justified by EXP129 and the cheap positional label pooler implementation is otherwise intact, so the correct next action is a clean rerun that consumes only the forward-routing fix.

hypothesis: If the main weakness of the fast pairwise scorer is that its label representations throw away token order by mean-pooling candidate tokens, then replacing that mean pool with a tiny position-aware attention pool should recover part of the candidate-token signal while keeping the scorer in the scalable non-autoregressive regime.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, loss-family changes, sampling changes, or packaging changes.
This is a clean rerun of EXP135 after fixing the forward-routing bug that sent candidate_pairwise_positional_interaction into the top-k dual hybrid branch on item-incoterms.
No scientific change relative to EXP132 or EXP135; the rerun exists only to recover the intended salt-side evidence.
Launched on 2026-05-09 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Completed on 2026-05-09 with global verdict: failed.
user-churn finished clearly better, user-ltv stayed inside its replay-aware neutral band, and item-incoterms fell far below the strict MRR baseline even after the forward-routing fix.
Conclusion: static position-aware label pooling does not recover enough salt-side candidate-token signal to keep this family on the active frontier.
